alternatives_considered: CLI hook to auto-generate metrics at session end — rejected: requires Go code, adds v0.7.0 scope, and runtime hooks don't exist yet
L.E.O. self-reports all metrics from recall at wrap-up — rejected: same memory-dependent pattern that caused the gap in the first place
Hybrid: fix skill now, CLI hook later — rejected by user: both fixes needed in v0.7.0
content.context: Post v0.6.0, zero metric docs existed despite the metrics-collection constraint. Investigation revealed the metric type was designed around L.E.O. self-reporting session data from recall — unreliable. The solution separates data production (L.E.O. writes structured docs at wrap-up) from data collection (external scripts read files).
content.decision: Metrics collection follows a three-tier model. Tier 1: external scripts read KB files (metadata + structured session-log docs). Tier 2: scripts parse doc content for semantic signals (errors, violations, quality). Tier 3: runtime telemetry (tokens, cost, duration) — out of scope until L.E.O. owns the runtime.
impact: metric type dropped from schema, replaced by session-log
session_id becomes mandatory on base schema — links all KB docs to their producing session
session-log docs stored in .leo/logs/, never loaded at boot, never rendered by adapters
decision type: alternatives_considered, impact, reversible become mandatory
Delegation enforcement inferred from profile mismatch (session.profile != task.profile), not self-reported
content.why: You cannot improve an orchestrator without metrics. But metrics must come from artifacts on disk, not from agent recall. L.E.O. produces logs, never consumes them. Monitoring is external — scripts, dashboards, SREs.
